The skill was largely updated in the previous session. Remaining gaps from conversation history still need to be filled.

Four specific gaps remain:

- Grand Slam Champions sorting by total titles descending, with ties resolved by most recent slam win.
- Grand Slam doubles link format using dual links like singles.
- Criteria for Matches to Watch Out For and promotion to Head-to-Head.
- Played opponents should move to Head-to-Head rather than the watch list.

Two more gaps need fixing.

The winner template currently uses plain text like "Birmingham (WTA 125) - Winner" instead of markdown links. This needs updating.

For ABS-CBN, the policy accepts full match replays from official broadcasters but rejects news segments. There is some conflict between the reject list and the Philippines section.

Five ABS-CBN full match replays were kept for Head-to-Head tracking.

The existing H2H opponent list needs updating to include Gauff and Rybakina with the correct sort order.

A quick routing table should be added for link format updates, watch list suggestions, and Grand Slam Champions re-sort.

The reference.md doubles Grand Slam template also needs to be created.

The video policy now distinguishes between actual tennis broadcasts and highlights. Full-match replays are permitted from official tour or slam sources and from rights-holding broadcasters globally. Fan channels including Tennis Stories, CueTV, Nice One, Guil Signe, stateofsport211, and Next Tennis Gems are rejected. News segments such as TV Patrol result clips, DZMM, and Play by Play on One Sports are also rejected.

One Sports is accepted only for full game highlights. Link order requires wtatennis.com first, then tennis.com for WTA singles, and wtatennis first, then official slam for Grand Slams in both singles and doubles. Top 20 Head-to-Head entries must use YouTube only and cannot include match lines. The last two matches should each receive a YouTube embed for WTA singles tournament runs. A global broadcaster search must precede omitting an embed. The existing H2H list also lacks a documented sort order.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Updated the `update-alex-eala-tracker` skill from the [prior tracker session](18dbac08-190d-4017-9b54-2dd57c8d6bbe) — rules that were applied to the blog post but not yet written into the skill.

### `SKILL.md`

- **Quick routing** — watch-list, link format, and Grand Slam Champions re-sort paths
- **Grand Slam doubles** — same dual-link format as singles (`wtatennis.com` first, `LD…` score IDs)
- **Grand Slam Champions sort** — by total Slam titles, then most recent title on ties
- **Promotion rule** — move anyone with a completed pro H2H off the watch list
- **Link format rules** — table for singles, titles, Slams, and both H2H section types
- **Watch-list workflow** — who qualifies, line format, when to skip or promote
- **H2H workflow** — remove from watch list after promotion; re-sort Slam champions when needed
- **Verify step** — link order, sort order, broadcaster-only embeds

### `reference.md`

- **Templates** — title runs with dual links; Grand Slam doubles block
- **Grand Slam Champions sort table** — current order (Swiatek → Ostapenko)
- **H2H opponent list** — added Gauff and Rybakina in sort order
- **Watch-list criteria** — no pro H2H yet; promote after first meeting
- **YouTube reject list** — added `LUWALHATI`
- **Philippines broadcasters** — One Sports highlights vs ABS-CBN full replays vs TV Patrol news clips

The skill now matches the conventions established while building the tracker: WTA-first links, global broadcaster search, official channels only (no fan reuploads), Top 20 H2H as embeds-only, and Slam champions sorted by major count.

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

The skill now covers what to do when a Top 20 opponent wins a major.

### New: **Slam-winner promotion workflow** (`SKILL.md`)

On every full refresh, check each **Other Top 20** opponent’s WTA profile for Slam singles titles. If they have one:

1. **Remove** them from Other Top 20  
2. **Move** to Grand Slam Champions with the full format (titles table, `**Matches:**` stat lines, YouTube under match lines)  
3. **Re-sort** Grand Slam Champions by title count  
4. **Update** `reference.md` opponent lists  

### Rule changes

- **New opponent rule:** Slam winners go straight to **Grand Slam Champions**, never Other Top 20 — even if they’re current top 20 (e.g. Gauff, Rybakina path).
- **First meeting after a Slam win:** same — Grand Slam Champions directly, not Other Top 20 first.
- **Doubles-only edge case:** stay in Other Top 20 until a singles H2H exists (Paolini is flagged in `reference.md` as a pending check).
- **Verify step:** no Slam title-holder should remain in Other Top 20 without reason.

### `reference.md`

Added an **H2H section placement** table mapping opponent status → section → format, plus a Paolini note as the doubles-only example.

---

_**User**_

add videos for grand slam doubles if available

---

```bash
verify() {
  for id in "$@"; do
    curl -s "https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=$id&format=json" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'$id | {d.get(\"author_name\")} | {d.get(\"title\")[:90]}')" 2>/dev/null || echo "$id | ERROR"
  done
}

for q in \
  "Eala Martins Australian Open 2026 doubles highlights" \
  "Alexandra Eala Ingrid Martins Aoyama Linette Australian Open 2026" \
  "Eala Lys Wimbledon 2025 doubles highlights" \
  "Eala Zarazua Roland Garros 2025 doubles highlights" \
  "Eala Zarazua French Open 2025 doubles beIN"; do
  echo "=== $q ==="
  enc=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('$q'))")
  curl -sL "https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=$enc" -A "Mozilla/5.0" | grep -oE 'watch\?v=[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11}' | head -8 | sort -u | while read line; do id=${line#watch?v=}; verify "$id"; done
  echo
done
```

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.parse, urllib.request, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

OFFICIAL = {'WTA','LTA','Wimbledon','Australian Open','US Open Tennis Championships','Roland-Garros','SPOTV ASIA','beIN SPORTS','beIN SPORTS Asia','Eurosport','Tennis Channel','Tennis Australia','One Sports','ABS-CBN News'}
REJECT = {'tennis stories','cuetv','nice one','guil signe','luwalhati','talking tennis','carley','mas jo sport','eala nation','highlight frenzy','kaguilb','sports updates'}

queries = [
  "Australian Open 2026 Eala Martins doubles highlights",
  "SPOTV Australian Open 2026 Eala Martins doubles",
  "Roland Garros 2025 Eala Zarazua doubles highlights SPOTV",
  "Roland Garros 2025 Eala Zarazua doubles round 2",
  "Wimbledon 2025 Eala Lys doubles SPOTV",
]

seen = set()
for q in queries:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        if vid in seen: continue
        a,t = info(vid)
        tl = t.lower(); al = a.lower()
        if any(r in al or r in tl for r in REJECT): continue
        if any(k in tl for k in ['double','doubles','martins','lys','zarazua','aoyama','linette','gleason','h/l','highlight','extended']) or a in OFFICIAL:
            seen.add(vid)
            print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:95]}')
    print()
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.parse, urllib.request, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

OFFICIAL = {'WTA','LTA','Wimbledon','Australian Open','US Open Tennis Championships','Roland-Garros','SPOTV ASIA','beIN SPORTS','beIN SPORTS Asia','Eurosport','Tennis Channel','Tennis Australia','One Sports','ABS-CBN News'}
REJECT = {'tennis stories','cuetv','nice one','guil signe','luwalhati','talking tennis','carley','mas jo sport','eala nation','highlight frenzy','kaguilb','sports updates'}

queries = [
  "Australian Open 2026 Eala Martins doubles highlights",
  "SPOTV Australian Open 2026 Eala Martins doubles",
  "Roland Garros 2025 Eala Zarazua doubles highlights SPOTV",
  "Roland Garros 2025 Eala Zarazua doubles round 2",
  "Wimbledon 2025 Eala Lys doubles SPOTV",
]

seen = set()
for q in queries:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        if vid in seen: continue
        a,t = info(vid)
        tl = t.lower(); al = a.lower()
        if any(r in al or r in tl for r in REJECT): continue
        if any(k in tl for k in ['double','doubles','martins','lys','zarazua','aoyama','linette','gleason','h/l','highlight','extended']) or a in OFFICIAL:
            seen.add(vid)
            print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:95]}')
    print()
PY
```

Specific content gaps stand out. The Player Profile lacks a WTA Doubles Matches link. Several tournaments are missing YouTube embeds, including Jingshan, Guadalajara, Ilkley, Canberra, Sao Paolo, and Coco Gauff. Grand Slam doubles matches have no videos. The page also omits many notable singles runs, such as Berlin 2026 and Indian Wells.

Doubles entries lack the dual WTA links that singles uses. Paolini's 2024 French Open title could be highlighted more prominently. The page shows no last-updated date, and the frontmatter date suggests possible staleness. Navigation suffers from a long scroll without jump links or an anchor menu. Heavy YouTube embeds slow down loading. Some H2H videos appear duplicated.

Several YouTube embeds are duplicated across different sections. The sorting logic differs between sections intentionally. Notable gaps include Fed Cup, BJK Cup, and Olympic results. The 2022 Junior US Open win is only mentioned in the Andreeva watch line. A summary of overall H2H record against slam champions is also missing.

SEO and accessibility could be improved with structured data. Lazy loading for YouTube embeds is worth considering. Technical cleanup includes trailing whitespace and a typo between Sao Paolo and Sao Paulo. Using Hugo's lastmod field would help track updates.

A brief stats callout at the top would let readers quickly see career highs and records. The watch list could split into "Slam legends" and "rising fixtures" for easier scanning. The introduction could link directly to section anchors.</details></think>

Here are practical improvements, grouped by impact.

## High impact (content & accuracy)

**1. Fill known gaps from your own rules**
- **Promote Jasmine Paolini** — French Open 2024 champion; she belongs in **Grand Slam Champions** once you decide how to handle the Rome doubles-only meeting (your skill already flags this).
- **Add missing 2026 runs** — The page stops at QF+ in several places, but 2026 likely has more worth noting (Indian Wells, Miami, Italian Open vs Rybakina, Berlin, etc.). Cross-check [WTA singles matches](https://www.wtatennis.com/players/330332/alexandra-eala/matches) for anything at QF or better that’s missing.
- **Coco Gauff H2H** — Only section with match lines but **no YouTube embeds**, despite Dubai and Indian Wells being high-profile. Worth a targeted search on WTA / broadcasters.

**2. Complete doubles parity**
- **Player Profile** — Add [WTA Doubles Matches](https://www.wtatennis.com/players/330332/alexandra-eala/matches?type=D) (in the skill, not on the page yet).
- **Best Performances → Doubles** — No `wtatennis.com` / partner links, unlike singles and Grand Slam doubles. Same dual-link format would make the section much more useful.
- **Grand Slam doubles videos** — Only Wimbledon 2025 has an embed; AO 2026 and RG 2025 are still link-only.

**3. Singles runs still missing highlights**
These blocks are link-only while similar runs have embeds: Jingshan, Guadalajara, Ilkley, Canberra 2025, Sao Paolo R16. Either keep searching under the global broadcaster policy or accept the gap — but right now the page feels uneven.

---

## Medium impact (UX & readability)

**4. Add a table of contents**
At ~445 lines with many embeds, a short jump menu after the intro would help a lot:

`Rankings · WTA Results · Grand Slams · vs Slam Champions · vs Top 20 · Watch List`

Hugo can auto-generate this if the theme supports `toc: true` in frontmatter — worth checking.

**5. “At a glance” snapshot**
A small block under the intro keeps casual readers from scrolling:

- Career highs (singles / doubles)
- WTA titles (2)
- Best Slam (US Open 2025 R2)
- Notable firsts (first Filipina in Slam main draw, first WTA 1000 SF, etc.)

That fits the scrapbook voice without turning it into a stats dashboard.

**6. Show when it was last updated**
Add `lastmod` in frontmatter or a one-line note (“Last updated: …”). A living tracker feels more trustworthy when readers know how fresh it is.

**7. Reduce duplicate embeds**
Same video appears in multiple sections (e.g. Abu Dhabi QF in **Best Performances** and **Alexandrova H2H**; Miami SF in **Best Performances**, **Swiatek**, and **Pegula**). Options:
- Keep embed once in the “main” section and link from H2H (“see Miami QF highlights above”), or
- Keep H2H embeds only and drop duplicates from tournament lists.

Less repetition = faster page and less visual noise.

---

## Lower impact (polish)

**8. Performance**
You have a lot of YouTube iframes on one URL. For older 2024–2025 runs, consider:
- Collapsible `<details>` sections per year, or
- Links to YouTube instead of embeds for deep archive matches

Worth testing on mobile — this page will be heavy.

**9. Small consistency fixes**
- “Sao **Paolo**” → “Sao **Paulo**”
- Trim stray blank lines (e.g. around Dubai 2026)
- **Madrid 2026** vs Swiatek — if she reached QF+, it may belong in Best Performances; if not, fine to omit per your QF threshold

**10. Watch list structure**
Last-name sort is good. You could split into two sub-lists for scanning:
- **Slam winners / legends** (Osaka, Sabalenka, Kerber, …)
- **Current tour crosspaths** (Navarro, Anisimova, Zheng, …)

Optional — only if the list keeps growing.

**11. SEO & discovery**
- `description` and tags are solid.
- A sentence in the intro linking to your update workflow or RSS (if you have one) helps returning readers.
- Junior US Open 2022 win could get a one-line **Pre-WTA milestones** note — only if you want junior context on this pro tracker.

---

## What’s already working well

- Clear voice and scrapbook framing
- Consistent link pattern (`wtatennis.com` first)
- Grand Slam Champions sorted by major count
- Other Top 20 / watch list sorted by last name
- Milestone sub-bullets (Manila, Miami, first title on grass) — these are the page’s personality

---

**If I were to prioritize three changes:** (1) at-a-glance + last updated, (2) TOC, (3) content pass for Gauff embeds, Paolini promotion, and doubles link parity.

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:8]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

queries = [
  "Coco Gauff Alex Eala Indian Wells 2026 WTA Match Highlights",
  "Coco Gauff Alex Eala Dubai 2026 WTA highlights",
  "Gauff Eala Indian Wells 2026 extended highlights",
  "Eala Martins Aoyama Linette Australian Open 2026 doubles SPOTV",
  "Eala Zarazua Roland Garros 2025 doubles SPOTV highlights",
  "Eala Zarazua French Open 2025 doubles WTA",
  "Zarazua Eala Roland Garros 2025 doubles beIN",
]
for q in queries:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        try:
            a,t = info(vid)
            al, tl = a.lower(), t.lower()
            if any(r in al for r in ['tennis stories','cuetv','nice one','guil signe','talking tennis','eala nation']):
                continue

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:8]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

queries = [
  "Coco Gauff Alex Eala Indian Wells 2026 WTA Match Highlights",
  "Coco Gauff Alex Eala Dubai 2026 WTA highlights",
  "Gauff Eala Indian Wells 2026 extended highlights",
  "Eala Martins Aoyama Linette Australian Open 2026 doubles SPOTV",
  "Eala Zarazua Roland Garros 2025 doubles SPOTV highlights",
  "Eala Zarazua French Open 2025 doubles WTA",
  "Zarazua Eala Roland Garros 2025 doubles beIN",
]
for q in queries:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        try:
            a,t = info(vid)
            al, tl = a.lower(), t.lower()
            if any(r in al for r in ['tennis stories','cuetv','nice one','guil signe','talking tennis','eala nation']):
                continue
            if 'play by play' in tl or 'tv patrol' in tl:
                continue
            print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:90]}')
        except: pass
    print()

import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:15]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:15]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

for q in [
  '"Coco Gauff vs. Alexandra Eala" Indian Wells 2026',
  'Coco Gauff Alexandra Eala 2026 Indian Wells Round 3 WTA',
  'BNP Paribas Open Gauff Eala 2026 Match Highlights',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        a,t = info(vid)
        print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t}')
    print()
PY

import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

for q in [
  'Dubai Duty Free Gauff Eala 2026 highlights',
  'Eala Zhang Queen London 2026 WTA highlights',
  'Eala Rybakina Berlin 2026 WTA Match Highlights',
  'Eala Tjen Abu Dhabi doubles 2026 WTA',
  'Gauff Eala Rome doubles 2025 WTA Match Highlights',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        try:
            a,t = info(vid)
            al,tl = a.lower(), t.lower()
            if any(r in al for r in ['tennis stories','cuetv','nice one','eala nation','talking tennis','tennis life']):
                continue
            if 'play by play' in tl or 'tv patrol' in tl:
                continue
            print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:90]}')
        except: pass
    print()

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

for q in [
  'Dubai Duty Free Gauff Eala 2026 highlights',
  'Eala Zhang Queen London 2026 WTA highlights',
  'Eala Rybakina Berlin 2026 WTA Match Highlights',
  'Eala Tjen Abu Dhabi doubles 2026 WTA',
  'Gauff Eala Rome doubles 2025 WTA Match Highlights',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        try:
            a,t = info(vid)
            al,tl = a.lower(), t.lower()
            if any(r in al for r in ['tennis stories','cuetv','nice one','eala nation','talking tennis','tennis life']):
                continue
            if 'play by play' in tl or 'tv patrol' in tl:
                continue
            print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:90]}')
        except: pass
    print()
PY

import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

for q in [
  'Gauff Eala Rome doubles 2025 WTA Match Highlights Panova',
  'Eala Gauff Pigato Birrell Rome doubles 2025 WTA',
  'Eala Zarazua Roland Garros 2025 doubles WTA Match Highlights',
  'Eala Martins Australian Open 2026 doubles WTA Match Highlights',
  'Rybakina Eala Berlin 2026 Round 2 WTA Match Highlights',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        try:
            a,t = info(vid)
            if any(r in a.lower() for r in ['sports superhuman','coach paul','highlight frenzy','tennis stories']):
                continue
            if 'gauff' in t.lower() and 'eala' in t.lower() or 'zarazua' in t.lower() and 'eala' in t.lower() or 'martins' in t.lower() and 'eala' in t.lower() or 'rybakina' in t.lower() and 'eala' in t.lower() and 'berlin' in t.lower():
                print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:95]}')
        except: pass
    print()

python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

for q in [
  'Gauff Eala Rome doubles 2025 WTA Match Highlights Panova',
  'Eala Gauff Pigato Birrell Rome doubles 2025 WTA',
  'Eala Zarazua Roland Garros 2025 doubles WTA Match Highlights',
  'Eala Martins Australian Open 2026 doubles WTA Match Highlights',
  'Rybakina Eala Berlin 2026 Round 2 WTA Match Highlights',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        try:
            a,t = info(vid)
            if any(r in a.lower() for r in ['sports superhuman','coach paul','highlight frenzy','tennis stories']):
                continue
            if 'gauff' in t.lower() and 'eala' in t.lower() or 'zarazua' in t.lower() and 'eala' in t.lower() or 'martins' in t.lower() and 'eala' in t.lower() or 'rybakina' in t.lower() and 'eala' in t.lower() and 'berlin' in t.lower():
                print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:95]}')
        except: pass
    print()

Earlier, Eala advanced to the second round when American Hailey Baptiste retired from their match with the Filipina ahead 6-4, 0-1.\n[...]\nAlso playing at a career-high ranking, World No. 40 Eala captured a back-and-forth first set against Baptiste after just about three-quarters of an hour, and the American lucky loser pulled the plug a game later. The World No. 39 had lost to Rebecca Sramkova in the final round of qualifying only to earn a second shot in the main draw, but could not continue after toughing out a seven-deuce service hold to start the second set.\n[...]\nafter the pre-tournament withdrawal of No. 15 seed and Doha champion Karolina Muchova, as well as 2024 Olympic champion Zheng Qinwen. She led 2021 US Open finalist Leylah Fernandez by a set, but\n[...]\nCanadian rallied to win 5-7, 7-5, 6-3 -- dropping Samsonova to 0-5 in three-set matches this season.\n[...]\nSara Bejlek and Alexandra Eala scored wins in their respective Dubai debut. Abu Dhabi champion Bejlek continued her hot streak with an efficient victory, while Eala advanced after her opponent's retirement. Seeded No. 2 in Guangzhou, she took advantage of her lofty draw status to reach her sixth career final without the loss of a set, and continued that trend against 2024 WTA Newcomer of the Year Sun, the former World No. 39 who came through qualifying to reach her second career final.\n[...]\nLi was 0-2 against Sun in their all-time head-to-head previously, but wrestled away control of their third match by winning a tight first set that lasted more than an hour.\n[...]\nThe American saved all five break points she faced in the first set -- she faced at least one in four service games -- and eventually flipped the script from 3-0 down in the tiebreak. She previously had four set points -- two with Sun serving down 5-4, and another two on the New Zealander's serve at 6-5 -- but couldn't convert them.\n[...]\nLi is the eighth American to win a singles title on the Hologic WTA Tour this year -- and first to win in Guangzhou since Sofia Kenin took the 2019 title.\n[...]\nAnn Li ended a four-year title drought by defeating Lulu Sun 7-6(6), 6-2 at the Guangzhou Open. Eala also won the 2025 Guadalajara 125 Open singles title by defeating Panna Udvardy in the final [4].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eGuadalajara Open Akron presented by Santander 2024 Scores | Latest Scores \u0026 Live Updates – WTA Official\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/2075/guadalajara/2024/scores\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eGuadalajara Open Akron presented by Santander 2024 Scores | Latest Scores \u0026 Live Updates – WTA Official\n\nGo back to the home page\n\n-\n\n10:05\n\n### Champions Reel: How Iva Jovic won Guadalajara 2025\n\n7mo ago\n\n04:59\n\n### Jovic captures Guadalajara crown, set to break Top 50\n\n7mo ago\n\n04:58\n\n### Jovic, 17, outlasts Bartunkova, 19, in Guadalajara semis\n\n7mo ago\n\n04:59\n\n### Arango makes Mexican magic again, into another WTA 500 final\n\n7mo ago\n\n00:50\n\n### Hear her roar! Arango turns defense into offense against Jacquemot\n\n7mo ago\n\n00:50\n\n### Hot shot: Bartunkova conjures backhand angle from nowhere in Guadalajara\n\n7mo ago\n\n00:39\n\n### Hot shot: Elsa Jacquemot's scrambling, leaping athleticism in Guadalajara\n\n7mo ago\n\n04:08\n\n### Bartunkova knocks out defending champ Frech in Guadalajara quarters\n\n7mo ago\n\n02:58\n\n### Arango stumps Stakusic's bid for two wins in one day in Guadalajara quarters\n\n7mo ago\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlexandra Eala\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexandra_Eala\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eThe 2024 season marked further progress, highlighted by her victory at the W100 Open Araba en Femenino in Vitoria-Gasteiz, Spain—her biggest ITF singles title to date. She also collected three ITF doubles trophies, partnering with Darja Semeņistaja to win the W50 Pune in India, and with Estelle Cascino to claim the W75 Open de Seine-et-Marne in Croissy-Beaubourg, France and the W100 Open Araba en Femenino in Spain. On the WTA Tour, Eala reached the quarterfinals of the Veneto Open, her best result of the year, and advanced to the semifinals of the WTA 125 Canberra Tennis International doubles event with Laura Pigossi.\n[...]\nDhabi, Guadalajara\n[...]\n25 Canberra\n[...]\nEala was\n[...]\nDuring her US Open debut, she defeated Clara Tauson in the first round, becoming the first Filipino player to achieve a match victory in a major tournament in the Open Era, but was subsequently defeated by Cristina Bucșa in the second round. Seeded second, she won her first WTA 125 title by defeating Panna Udvardy at the Guadalajara 125 Open final, becoming the first Filipino to achieve this milestone. Eala then lost to Janice Tjen during the quarterfinals of the SP Open. She competed at the Jingshan Open, where eventual champion Lulu Sun defeated her during the semifinals. At the Suzhou Open, she reached the quarterfinals, where she lost to champion Viktorija Golubic. She later teamed up with Nadiia Kichenok to reach the doubles semifinals at the WTA 250 Guangzhou Open, falling to eventual champions Katarzyna Piter and Janice Tjen.\n[...]\n| Result | W–L | Date | Tournament | Surface | Opponent | Score |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| Win | 1–0 | Sep 2025 | Guadalajara 125 Open, Mexico | Hard | Panna Udvardy | 1–6, 7–5, 6–3 |\n| Win | 2–0 | Jun 2026 | Birmingham Open, United Kingdom | Grass | Nikola Bartunková | 5–7\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eEala/Semenistaja vs. Falkowska/Kubka | Quarterfinals Polish Open 2024 | WTA Official\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/2087/warsaw-125/2024/scores/LD007\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eEala/Semenistaja vs. Falkowska/Kubka | Quarterfinals Polish Open 2024 | WTA Official\n\nGo back to the home page\n\n-\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eMaria vs. Eala | Round of 16 Guadalajara 125 Open 2024 | WTA Official\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/2098/guadalajara-125/2024/scores/LS010\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eMaria vs. Eala | Round of 16 Guadalajara 125 Open 2024 | WTA Official\n\nEN\n\nEnglish\n\nEspañol\n\nGo back to the home page\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

She also collected three ITF doubles trophies, partnering with Darja Semeņistaja to win the W50 Pune in India, and with Estelle Cascino to claim the W75 Open de Seine-et-Marne in Croissy-Beaubourg, France and the W100 Open Araba en Femenino in Spain. On the WTA Tour, Eala reached the quarterfinals of the Veneto Open, her best result of the year, and advanced to the semifinals of the WTA 125 Canberra Tennis International doubles event with Laura Pigossi.\n[...]\nEala began 2025 by reaching the semifinals of the WTA 125 Canberra International. Ranked No. 140, Eala was awarded a wildcard for the Miami Open, where she defeated Jeļena Ostapenko, Madison Keys, and Iga Świątek, before losing to Jessica Pegula in the semifinals. Following these results, Eala became the first Filipino to reach a WTA 1000 event semifinal, the first Filipino woman to defeat a major champion at a tour-level event in the Open Era, and the first wildcard in history to defeat three major champions in straight sets at a single WTA event. Batch of tracker improvements is done. Summary:

### Tracker (`alex-eala-pro-career-tracker.md`)

**At a glance** — Added under the intro (career highs, 2 titles, best Slam, notable firsts).

**Player Profile** — Added [WTA Doubles Matches](https://www.wtatennis.com/players/330332/alexandra-eala/matches?type=D).

**2026 singles** — Added **Berlin** (R16, Vekic win + WTA embed) and **London/Queen's** (R16 vs Jovic, R32 vs Zhang + embed).

**Doubles** — All entries now have `wtatennis.com` links (exit round + sub-bullets). Abu Dhabi 2026 has QF/SF WTA embeds.

**Coco Gauff H2H** — Dubai QF embed (`Z-NN1Y1lknY`) added. **Indian Wells:** no official WTA/broadcaster upload found — stat line only.

**Embed dedup (H2H wins)** — Removed duplicates from tournament blocks:
- Abu Dhabi: Alexandrova QF → H2H only
- Dubai: Gauff QF → Gauff H2H only (Cirstea QF kept)
- Miami: Świątek SF + Pegula SF → H2H only (Miami block has no embeds now)
- RG 2026 / Wimbledon 2025 singles → Jovic / Krejčíková H2H only

**Grand Slam doubles** — Wimbledon embed unchanged. **AO 2026** and **RG 2025** still link-only (no verified official doubles highlights).

**Misc** — Fixed Sao Paulo typo; set `lastmod`.

### Skill updates

- **Paolini:** Clarified she stays in Other Top 20 (doubles meeting only; doubles-Slam titles don’t promote).
- **Embed dedup rule** added to `SKILL.md` and `reference.md`.
- **Doubles link format** documented in `reference.md`.

### Still to watch

1. **Berlin R16 vs Rybakina** — Listed as R16 with the Vekic win; update after that match if you want the Rybakina result/embed.
2. **Indian Wells Gauff** — Re-check when BNP/WTA posts a package.
3. **AO 2026 / RG 2025 doubles** — Same if official highlights appear later.
